requirements.title: Math Constants
requirements.description: The math module shall define double-precision constants for π, π/2, π/4, and e.
requirements.rationale: Pre-defined constants avoid repeated magic numbers and ensure consistent precision across all math operations in the library.

requirements.title: Vector Types
requirements.description: The math module shall define 2D and 3D vector types in double, float, and int32 precisions.
requirements.rationale: Multiple precision variants allow embedded applications to choose the appropriate trade-off between precision and memory/computation cost for their target platform.

requirements.title: Vector Coordinate Access
requirements.description: Each vector type shall support both named Cartesian field access and raw array indexing.
requirements.rationale: Dual access via named fields (x, y, z) and raw array indexing enables both readable application code and loop-based generic algorithms over vector components.

requirements.title: Quaternion Type
requirements.description: The math module shall define a real quaternion type with [s, i, j, k] component order in double precision.
requirements.rationale: Quaternions provide singularity-free rotation representation, which is critical for attitude determination and control in embedded navigation and robotics applications.

requirements.title: Vector Addition
requirements.description: The math module shall compute component-wise vector addition for each vector type.
requirements.rationale: Vector addition is a fundamental linear algebra operation used in position accumulation, force summation, and signal combination.

requirements.title: Vector Subtraction
requirements.description: The math module shall compute component-wise vector subtraction for each vector type.
requirements.rationale: Vector subtraction is used for computing displacements, error vectors, and relative quantities between two points or measurements.

requirements.title: Vector Scalar Multiplication
requirements.description: The math module shall compute component-wise scalar multiplication for each vector type.
requirements.rationale: Scalar multiplication is used for scaling vectors by gain factors, time steps, or unit conversions.

requirements.title: Vector Dot Product
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the dot product for each vector type.
requirements.rationale: The dot product computes projection and angle-related quantities, which are fundamental to geometry, physics, and signal processing.

requirements.title: 2D Cross Product
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the pseudoscalar z-component of the 2D cross product.
requirements.rationale: The 2D cross product (pseudoscalar z-component) determines signed area and winding direction, which is useful for 2D geometry and path planning.

requirements.title: 3D Cross Product
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the 3D cross product returning an orthogonal vector.
requirements.rationale: The 3D cross product computes torque, angular momentum, and surface normals, which are fundamental to 3D kinematics and dynamics.

requirements.title: Squared L2 Norm
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the squared L2 norm for each vector type.
requirements.rationale: The squared norm avoids a square-root computation, providing an efficient magnitude comparison and normalization building block for resource-constrained targets.

requirements.title: Quaternion Addition
requirements.description: The math module shall compute component-wise quaternion addition.
requirements.rationale: Quaternion addition is used in interpolation schemes (e.g., SLERP numerator) and filter state updates.

requirements.title: Quaternion Subtraction
requirements.description: The math module shall compute component-wise quaternion subtraction.
requirements.rationale: Quaternion subtraction is used for computing rotation error and difference quaternions in attitude estimation.

requirements.title: Quaternion Scalar Multiplication
requirements.description: The math module shall compute component-wise quaternion scalar multiplication.
requirements.rationale: Scalar multiplication is used for scaling quaternion corrections and blending interpolation weights.

requirements.title: Hamilton Product
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the right-handed Hamilton product of two quaternions.
requirements.rationale: The Hamilton product composes rotations, which is the core operation for attitude propagation and frame transformation in navigation systems.

requirements.title: Quaternion Conjugate
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the conjugate of a quaternion by negating the vector part.
requirements.rationale: The conjugate of a unit quaternion is its inverse rotation, providing an efficient way to compute reverse transformations without division.

requirements.title: Quaternion Reciprocal
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the multiplicative inverse of a quaternion as conj(q) divided by the squared norm.
requirements.rationale: The reciprocal generalizes the inverse to non-unit quaternions, which is needed for quaternion division and certain filter update equations.

requirements.title: Quaternion Squared Norm
requirements.description: The math module shall compute the squared L2 norm of a quaternion.
requirements.rationale: The squared norm is the denominator in the reciprocal computation and provides an efficient normalization check without requiring a square root.
